fhir_verification_notes: GET /metadata returned HTTP 200 + valid CapabilityStatement (FHIR 4.0.1, publisher=AETNA, Da Vinci PDex Plan-Net). GET /Practitioner?_count=1 returned HTTP 401 — resource queries require OAuth2 client credentials registered at developerportal.aetna.com. Metadata endpoint is open; data endpoints are gated.
tic_notes: Main MRF portal page. Per-state variant exists (e.g. https://transparency.aetna.com/MRF_COLORADO). Landing page at https://www.aetna.com/insurance-producer/transparency-in-coverage.html.
directory_notes: Underlying DocFind tool at aetna.com/docfind. Aetna.com is protected by Akamai Bot Manager; direct scraping blocked.
stedi_hint: Primary payer ID: 60054. Stedi network ID: HPQRS. Aliases include: AETNA, 10004, 10397, 4500, 60054MA, 60055, 6400.
notes: Aetna (CVS Health) offers separate Medicare and commercial provider directory FHIR products. Medicare Bulk FHIR provider directory also available. Commercial provider directory queries require registered OAuth2 credentials; the /metadata endpoint is publicly reachable. Developer portal: https://developerportal.aetna.com/

fhir_verification_notes: GET /metadata returned HTTP 200 + valid CapabilityStatement (FHIR 4.0.1, publisher='Cigna, Inc.', 9 supported resource types including Practitioner, PractitionerRole, Organization, InsurancePlan). GET /Practitioner?_count=1 returned HTTP 200 + FHIR Bundle (searchset) with live practitioner data (e.g. NPI 1710975354). Fully public — no auth required for directory queries.
tic_notes: CMS Federal Transparency in Coverage machine-readable files index. Files in JSON; can be up to 1 TB each. Monthly updates. System requirements documented at https://static.cigna.com/assets/cignaaccess/en-us/pdf/MRF-System-Requirements.pdf.
directory_notes: Main public provider directory. Uses consumer codes for plan type (e.g. HDC011 for SAR plan). Site is protected; automated scraping likely blocked. Developer portal: https://developer.cigna.com/
stedi_hint: Primary payer ID: 62308. Stedi network ID: HGJLR. Aliases include: CIGNA, 10050, 10789, 10790, 86033, 86033MA, 62308MA.
notes: Cigna FHIR Provider Directory API is fully CMS-compliant and publicly accessible without auth — the most open of the six payers tested. Conforms to HL7 Da Vinci PDex Plan-Net IG.

fhir_verification_notes: GET /api/metadata returned HTTP 200 + valid CapabilityStatement (FHIR 4.0.1, publisher='Humana', software='HumanaFHIR', server at eastus2-fhir-interop.humana.com). GET /api/Practitioner?_count=1 returned HTTP 200 + FHIR Bundle with 925,492 total practitioners and live practitioner data (NPI 1861526451). Fully public — no auth required for directory queries.
tic_notes: Plan and Issuer Price Transparency machine-readable files index. No access restrictions. Monthly updates required by federal rule. Out-of-network files also available via same portal with fileType=outofnetwork.
directory_notes: Public-facing find-a-doctor tool. Page timed out during automated fetch, consistent with WAF/bot protection. Developer portal: https://developers.humana.com/
stedi_hint: Primary payer ID: 61101. Stedi network ID: UYORK. Aliases include: HUMANA, 00041, 10100, 61101MA, 65031, 65031MA, 73288.
notes: Humana FHIR Provider Directory API (fhir.humana.com) is fully CMS-compliant and publicly accessible without auth. Conforms to HL7 Da Vinci PDex Plan-Net IG. Developer portal note: 'website will be retired soon; FHIR products migrating to new site.'

fhir_verification_notes: No publicly documented base URL found. UHC confirms they have a Provider Directory API supporting CMS-mandated Plan-Net (with public/non-authenticated directory endpoints per their documentation), but the actual production base URL is disclosed only after OneHealthcare ID registration and org approval through the Vendor Portal. URL pattern referenced in docs: https://[payer].fhir.flex.optum.com/ but specific provider directory host not confirmed. Cannot verify without registration.
tic_notes: Primary public MRF index. Files published in JSON (CMS schema v2.0). Monthly updates. Self-funded opt-out files at https://private-transparency-in-coverage.uhc.com/. Additional info at https://www.uhc.com/united-for-reform/health-reform-provisions/transparency-in-coverage-rule.
directory_notes: Main public provider search. Returned HTTP 403 during automated fetch — Akamai/WAF protection. Provider lookup also at https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/find-a-provider-referral-directory.html for professionals.
stedi_hint: Primary payer ID: 87726. Stedi network ID: KMQTZ. Aliases include: UHC, UHCMP, 00198, 10002, 10122, 77082, 87726MA, 87726E.
notes: UHC confirms public (no user-auth) directory endpoints exist per CMS mandate, but base URL not publicly documented without org registration. Developer portal: https://www.uhc.com/legal/interoperability-apis. Note: CMS clarified that API keys for app tracking are allowed; end-user auth is not.

brand: Elevance Health (formerly Anthem) / Blue Cross Blue Shield affiliates
fhir_verification_notes: Base URL pattern documented in Anthem Interoperability API documentation (wellpoint.com PDF). Attempted fetches of /frmlry/metadata and /prvdr_dir/metadata both returned HTTP 403 Forbidden. The 'unregistered' path prefix is intended for public CMS-mandate endpoints but returned access denied during testing — may require specific path suffix not publicly confirmed, or may be geo/IP restricted. Registered path pattern (requires auth): https://totalview.healthos.elevancehealth.com/resources/registered/AnthemBlueCross/api/v1/fhir. Cannot confirm FHIR response.
tic_notes: Main MRF landing page. Table of Contents index at https://www.anthem.com/machine-readable-file/search/ — also supports EIN search. Files in CMS-defined JSON schema, updated monthly. Anthem is the Elevance Health brand for BCBS-affiliated plans across multiple states.
directory_notes: Public-facing provider search. Page timed out during automated fetch, consistent with WAF/Akamai bot protection. Developer portal: https://www.anthem.com/developers. Provider Directory API access request form at https://www.anthem.com/developers/provider-directory-api-request.
stedi_hint: Anthem Blue Cross CA primary payer ID: 040 (Stedi ID: LAESW). National/commercial varies by state BCBS affiliate. Common aliases: 00040, 10051, 84103, CABC. Contact Stedi or clearinghouse for state-specific IDs.
notes: Elevance Health (Anthem/Wellpoint) operates Medicaid, commercial, and Medicare plans across multiple states under BCBS license. API documentation at https://www.wellpoint.com/developers. FHIR endpoint likely behind Elevance HealthOS platform (totalview.healthos.elevancehealth.com); 403 encountered on tested unregistered paths.

fhir_verification_notes: No publicly documented FHIR PDEX Plan-Net base URL found. Kaiser's developer portal (developer.kp.org) lists FHIR APIs but all require app registration and credentials. The API catalog references HL7 FHIR R4 but their CMS-mandate provider directory endpoint URL is not published openly. Kaiser discloses machine-readable JSON files (providers.json, plans.json, drugs.json) per CMS regs, but these are not FHIR-formatted. Cannot verify a FHIR Plan-Net endpoint.
tic_notes: Transparency in Coverage page with region-specific MRF links. Regional MRF pages exist: Southern CA at https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/southern-california/front-door/machine-readable. Files in CMS JSON schema. Kaiser serves: CA (NorCal/SoCal), OR, WA, CO, GA, HI, MD, VA, DC.
directory_notes: Kaiser is a closed HMO system; provider directory is primarily for current/prospective members. Public-facing search available at https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/find-a-doctor but detailed search and scheduling require member login. Page returned 404 on one path variation tested; site showed maintenance page on another. Bot/WAF protection likely in place.
stedi_hint: Regional payer IDs (not a single national ID). NorCal: 94135. SoCal: 94285. Northwest (OR/WA): 86143. Hawaii: 91617. Kaiser requires direct clearinghouse enrollment; check with clearinghouse for current routing.
notes: Kaiser Permanente is an integrated HMO with region-specific payer IDs. FHIR Provider Directory API requires developer registration at developer.kp.org — no publicly accessible Plan-Net endpoint confirmed. Machine-readable provider data published per CMS regs in JSON format (not Plan-Net FHIR). Technical info: https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/northern-california/pages/technical-information.
